Variable tree

Provides a PyQt5 model for storing variables in a GUI.

This custom model, VariableTreeModel, is used for storing variables and their typed tokens in a hierarchical structure suitable for display in a GUI.

The VariableTreeModel class extends QtGui.QStandardItemModel and adds functionality for loading data from a dictionary of variables and a dictionary of typed tokens per variable. It also emits a signal, top_item_checked, whenever a new top-level item is checked.

Author: Alberto Rodriguez Fernandez

class VariableTreeModel(parent=None)[source]

Stores the variables in a model

To be used as a model for one or more views in the GUI.

Signals:
top_item_checked(QModelIndex): Emitted when a new top level item

is checked.

load_data(variables: Dict[str, Variable], typed_token_per_variable: Dict[str, Set[str]]) → None[source]

Loads the data into the model.

Parameters

  • variables (Dict[str, variable.Variable]) – Top level items to be added.

  • typed_token_per_variable (Dict[str, Set[str]]) – The keys include the variables used as top level items. The values contain the sub items for each top level item.

_set_check_state(item: QStandardItem, state: CheckState)[source]

Sets the check state for an item.

It also stores that state to be used later. This is particulary useful when the checkbox of an item is clicked, in that case the checkState changes before the clicked signal is emitted so information about the previous state is no longer available.

Parameters

  • item (QtGui.QStandardItem) – Item to change.

  • state (QtCore.Qt.CheckState) – The new state.

check_item(index: QModelIndex)[source]

Handles the check state changes.

The state changes are handled differently depending on the type of item, for more information check:

  • Top level: _check_top_level_item()

  • Sub items: _check_sub_item()

Parameters

index (QtCore.QModelIndex) – corresponds to the item whose state changes.

_check_top_level_item(index: QModelIndex)[source]

Handles the check state changes for top level items.

One and only one top level item must be checked at any time. If that item has children then the first children is automatically checked. This ensures that there is always at least one sub item checked.

Parameters

index (QtCore.QModelIndex) – corresponds to the item whose state changes.

Signals:

top_item_checked: Emitted when a new top level item is checked.

_check_sub_item(index: QModelIndex)[source]

Handles the check state changes for sub items.

There must be always at least one sub item checked. This means that checking an item is always allowed. For the unchecking operation a check needs to be performed to ensure that there is at least one other subitem checked.

Parameters

index (QtCore.QModelIndex) – corresponds to the item whose state changes.

get_checked_items() → Dict[str, List[str]][source]

Returns the checked items.

Returns

The keys are the top level elements. The

values are the checked sub items.

Return type

Dict[str, List[str]]

_get_checked_sub_items(top_level_item: QStandardItem) → List[str][source]

Returns the checked sub items for a top level item.

Parameters

top_level_item (QtGui.QStandardItem) – top level item whose sub items are going to be queried.

Returns

data of all the subitems that are checked.

Return type

List[str]
